label: UPSTREAM_OBJECT_UNAVAILABLE_DATA_CONFIRMED_REPRESENTED_ONLY
description: Contract defining the single documented-exception status for a GDELT 1.0 daily raw object that is officially data-confirmed but unavailable from the raw-events endpoint. Contract only; NO runtime behavior is implemented by this file or this turn.
scope_match_requirement: The documented-exception status may apply ONLY to the exact quintuple chunk_2022 + 2022-11-10 + 20221110.export.CSV.zip + label + (catalog_md5 AND catalog_filesize_bytes). Any other 404 (other date, other chunk, neighboring date) must NOT receive this label and must halt under existing runner behavior.
runtime_note: Runtime documented-exception support is implemented in scripts/run_lane2_gdelt1_full_daily_count_build.py (run_chunk_build): on a genuine 404 matching the exact committed quintuple (chunk_id + date + raw_filename + label + md5/size) it records a documented-exception entry and continues, WITHOUT fetching/recovering/parsing rows, WITHOUT treating the date as a no-data gap, and WITHOUT amending KNOWN_SUBSTRATE_GAPS or reducing expected_file_count. Every other 404 (other date, other chunk, wrong filename) and every non-404 failure still halts with FetchFailure. The post-support runner blob is recorded in the implementation commit/report (not embedded here to avoid a self-referential pin). merge_gate_open remains false: the merge-gate machinery (cross-chunk disclosure table + pre-merge conformance + a fresh labeled chunk_2022 run) is not yet complete.
known_substrate_gaps_relationship: This documented-exception mechanism is DISTINCT from KNOWN_SUBSTRATE_GAPS (the four 2014 no-data dates). 2022-11-10 must NEVER be added to KNOWN_SUBSTRATE_GAPS and is NOT a no-data gap.
